theme: Basket-13 catalyst funnel — event-driven special situations (diagnostic)
regime: NEUTRAL macro (regime score 0.5475, VIX ~16.8, 2s10s +27bp, growth+inflation both accelerating) sitting under a late-cycle, fully-invested, low-vol melt-up with a live oil/inflation tail and an UNUSUALLY OPEN US M&A/regulatory window (Ferguson FTC / Assefi DOJ remedy-friendly; 9 of 12 2025 challenges settled, not blocked) that may narrow after the Nov-2026 midterms.
risk_stance: Constructive-but-selective on HARD-DATED event arbitrage right now. The regime confirms the structural-special-sits tilt: the deal/forced-seller machine is hot and the regulator is permissive, so signed all-cash closes and board-approved spins are the fattest, cleanest lane and I will size INTO genuinely dated, well-floored binaries. The two disciplines the regime imposes: (1) the FDA/biotech binaries are the THINNEST-edge lane (run-up priced 4-8wks ahead) AND a single shared risk-on/off factor, so I treat the eight of them as ONE bet and demand a verified hard date + real cash floor before sizing; (2) with BofA FMS cash at a 3.9% sell-signal, ~50% net-OW equities and AI-capex the dominant under-the-tape factor, there is little dry powder to absorb a shock, so I enforce resolution-driver independence and refuse to let the biotech cluster dominate the book. Net: aggressive on the purest standalone, hard-dated, cash-floored asymmetries; patient (stage/watch) on everything undated or soft-dated; and explicitly correlation-capped on the FDA cluster.
correlation_memo: ONE SHARED FACTOR dominates the funnel: eight of seventeen names (BBIO, AQST, ZYME, VRDN, EYPT, CELC, AMLX, VIR) are FDA / clinical binaries that all resolve on the same risk-on/off axis (biotech beta + FDA approval sentiment + the regime's identified 'thinnest-edge, run-up-priced' PDUFA lane). Seating many of them is mathematically ONE bet, not eight — and per the §1.7 independence discipline (BofA FMS cash 3.9% sell-signal, little dry powder, AI-capex the dominant under-tape factor) I will NOT let this cluster carry the book: at most 2-3 of the highest-floor members (BBIO franchise-floor, CELC/EYPT cash-floor) get real size, the rest stay scaled/waiting. Within the cluster the sub-correlations matter too: VRDN/CELC are oncology/ophthalmology one-offs while AMLX/VIR/EYPT/ZYME share trial-execution and 'clinical-attrition' risk. The PUREST STANDALONE ASYMMETRIES — the names whose resolution driver is genuinely orthogonal to both biotech beta and to each other — are the idiosyncratic/forced-seller set: FIP (a signed power-asset sale + FERC clock), AAUC (a single China-NDRC sovereign-clearance binary), PRX.AS (Tencent/China NAV + an EU forced-seller clock), KBR/LBTYK/KDP (independent spin/Form-10 mechanics), CLVT/BLCO (idiosyncratic deleveraging/parent-overhang). FIP and AAUC are the two cleanest, most-independent hard-dated bets in the book; UNF is the one remaining merger-arb but its driver (FTC) overlaps with no other name yet its spread has collapsed. Build the book around 2-3 independent idiosyncratic closes + a CAPPED biotech sleeve, never a biotech-heavy book that is secretly a single levered bet on the next risk-on tape.
director_memo: THE HEADLINE — ANSWERED: TWO names cleared the never-before-seen triple. Across 17 event-driven candidates, exactly two — BBIO and FIP — simultaneously earned CRO verdict A, CRO conviction 5, AND a Director score >=80 (BBIO 84, FIP 83). The priced-quality funnels have NEVER produced this band in 407+ names; the catalyst funnel produced it on the first event-driven run, which is the structural point: these are DATED EVENTS, not compounders, so a name can legitimately be table-pounding (huge live-to-target spread + hard floor + imminent forcing function) in a way a fairly-priced quality stock never is. The raw ingredients were abundant — nine names carry CRO verdict A and seven carry CRO conviction 5 — but the Director-80 gate is deliberately hard, and it bound in two ways. (1) The Skeptic's conviction_cap is the dominant filter: of the seven CRO-5 names, the Skeptic left the FULL cap only on BBIO and FIP (both CONFIRMED, no load-bearing correction); it cut VRDN to 65 (false 'no-dilution' floor + refuted 'unrelated SC failure' dislocation), CELC to 75 (soft fundamental floor), ZYME to 60 (royalty-note-encumbered floor), and held EYPT at 4/80 (safety-only DSMC + two-leg LUGANO/LUCIA structure). So five of the seven CRO-5 names were hard-capped below 80 before I scored them. (2) BBIO and FIP survived the cap AND survived my own scoring: BBIO is a PDUFA sitting on a POSITIVE Phase 3 with a profitable-franchise floor ($940M cash + already-approved acoramidis + $500M buyback bid) and ~4:1 asymmetry — about as de-risked as a regulatory binary gets; FIP is a SIGNED, FINANCED $1.52B asset sale (MARA noteholder consent already launched) that mechanically cures a distressed balance sheet, ~4.6:1, with the only live risk being regulatory TIMING, not deal economics. Neither could be killed on its load-bearing facts. They are the two table-pounding event-driven entries the sleeve was built to find. Everything else fell short on at least one axis: AAUC (79), EYPT (78), CELC (74) are A-grade and high-conviction but Skeptic-capped just under 80; VRDN/ZYME/VIR/AMLX are A-verdicts the Skeptic cut for a real flaw; UNF/AQST/PRX.AS/BLCO/LBTYK/CLVT/KDP are B-verdicts (undated, soft-floored, or spread-collapsed). SEATING PLAN under the §1.7 independence + 3.9%-cash discipline: enter-now the two purest independent closes (BBIO, FIP); scale into AAUC (sovereign binary, independent) and a CAPPED biotech sleeve (EYPT, CELC) plus PRX.AS for its fortress floor; wait-for-date on VRDN/ZYME/VIR/AMLX/LBTYK/KBR; pass on the undated/soft-floored tail (UNF spread gone, AQST/KDP/BLCO undated, CLVT weakest floor + AI-secular). Do NOT build a biotech-heavy book — eight of these are one bet on the next risk-on tape; the two triple-clearers (BBIO franchise-floored biotech, FIP idiosyncratic close) are usefully independent of each other.

AAUC.bull_thesis: AAUC is a signed, friendly, all-cash merger-arb: Zijin Gold International agreed (definitive arrangement, 2026-01-26) to buy Allied Gold at C$44.00/share cash, ~C$5.5B, a 27% premium. At USD/CAD ~1.4146 the deal value is ~$31.08 USD vs a live $25.64 — a ~21% gross spread on a deal just 37 days from a HARD, mutual-agreement-only outside date of 2026-07-29. The runway is almost fully cleared: Investment Canada Act approval is DONE (completing the Canadian review), ECOWAS and COMESA competition clearances are DONE, and African host-country approvals are obtained or in advanced stages. The ONLY load-bearing remaining condition is China's NDRC outbound-investment approval. If NDRC clears before Jul-29 — the parties' base case, and the market's (the stock held near deal value at C$37.31 even on the delay news) — the spread converges to ~$31 USD for +21%. This is a board-recommended, all-cash deal with no financing-out, no CVR, no stock-ratio risk, into a record-high gold-price backdrop that strengthens Zijin's strategic motive to own the ounces. The wide spread is paying you for a single, identifiable sovereign-approval binary, not for a broken or undated process.
AAUC.bear_thesis: The entire residual risk is one sovereign, non-economic gatekeeper: China's NDRC, which has already DELAYED approval (the reason May→July extension happened), explicitly questioning (1) the 27% premium Zijin is paying — Beijing scrutinizing outbound capital outflow — and (2) Mali political risk, where ~half of Allied's output sits and where the military junta has arrested foreign mining executives and forcibly renegotiated contracts. This is the worst kind of arb condition: third-party, single-binary, and driven by capital-control/geopolitical judgment rather than a clearable checklist. The outside date is mutually-hard ('any further extension requires the parties' mutual agreement') — if NDRC isn't done by Jul-29, an NDRC-blocked Zijin can let the deal lapse. On a break, the premium evaporates: the stock derates toward its standalone, Mali-discounted value (~undisturbed C$34-35 less a risk haircut), i.e., the low-to-mid $20s USD. Mali concentration is the single point of failure that simultaneously drives the discount AND NDRC's hesitation — correlated risk. You are short a sovereign-rejection put for a 21% upside against a ~10% downside, and the spread is wide precisely because that put is live.
AAUC.sop_bull: Event-win value = the contractual cash consideration. C$44.00/share x USD/CAD 0.7065 (1/1.4146, Jun-2026) = ~$31.08 USD per share at close. No discounting needed beyond a small time-value/odds haircut to the 2026-07-29 outside date; the dossier target of $31.54 (using a slightly stronger ~1.395 CAD) brackets the same figure. Bull SoP ~ $31.00-31.54 USD = the deal value received in cash on completion.
AAUC.sop_bear: Break floor = standalone gold-miner value, derived from the undisturbed pre-deal price (the C$44 was a 27% premium to the 30-day undisturbed average, implying undisturbed ~C$34-35 ≈ ~$24-25 USD), then haircut for Mali single-jurisdiction risk (~50% of output) but offset upward by record-high gold prices that lift the underlying ounce value. The market's own read on the delay (held at C$37.31 ≈ ~$26.4 USD) sits above the break floor, so $23.00 USD is a conservative break floor — undisturbed USD-equivalent with a Mali risk-off haircut. Downside is bounded; a break strips the premium, it does not impair the gold ounces, and there is no going-concern/covenant/dilution risk.
AAUC.sop_breakdown: Probability-weighted of the two event outcomes. Assign ~65% to a clean NDRC clearance and close at the cash target ($31.08) and ~35% to an NDRC rejection / Jul-29 lapse to the standalone break floor ($23.00): 0.65 x 31.08 + 0.35 x 23.00 = $28.25; trimming the close-probability for the live, unresolved NDRC overhang and time-value to the outside date yields a base-case ~$27.4 USD — essentially the live price, i.e., the market is pricing ~50-55% close odds at this spread. Bull leg (deal): ~$31.08. Bear leg (break): ~$23.00. Underlying is a real cash-generative gold miner into record bullion, so the floor is hard, not theoretical.
AAUC.risk_reward: Upside-to-target +$5.44 (+21%, $25.64 -> $31.08) vs downside-to-floor -$2.64 (-10%, $25.64 -> $23.00). Raw asymmetry ~2.06x; on the dossier's $31.54/$23.00 marks ~2.78x. A defined-risk, fat-spread sovereign-binary arb: the wide spread compensates for live NDRC-rejection probability, and the floor caps the loss near 10%.
AAUC.catalyst_summary: Signed all-cash C$44 Zijin takeover, last gate = China NDRC approval before a hard 2026-07-29 outside date; live $25.64 vs ~$31 USD cash target with a ~$23 standalone floor — defined-risk ~2x merger-arb, gated on one sovereign clearance.
AAUC.dated_milestone: 2026-07-29 mutually-hard outside date for deal completion (extended once from ~May-2026); the single outstanding condition is China NDRC outbound-investment approval. Canada/ICA, ECOWAS, COMESA, and African host-country approvals already obtained.
AAUC.forcing_function: The 2026-07-29 outside date — 'any further extension requires the parties' mutual agreement.' Either NDRC clears and the deal closes at C$44 cash (re-rate to ~$31 USD), or it lapses/breaks and the stock derates to its ~$23 standalone floor. The NDRC decision is the trigger; the outside date is the wall that forces resolution.
AAUC.consensus_delta: This is not a mispriced-narrative story so much as a binary-odds disagreement. The ~21% gross spread, 37 days from a hard outside date, implies the market is pricing only ~50-55% odds of a clean close — extreme skepticism that NDRC clears, anchored on the visible delay and Mali headlines. The bull view is that NDRC's concerns (premium + Mali) are negotiating/process friction on a deal Zijin WANTS (strategic gold ounces into a record bullion market) and that all non-China approvals are already secured, making a clearance-by-Jul-29 more likely than a coin flip. If true, the market is over-discounting a sovereign clearance that ultimately rubber-stamps a strategically-motivated, fully-financed cash deal. The risk to that view is genuine: NDRC is non-economic and capital-control-driven, so 'Zijin wants it' does not guarantee Beijing approves the outflow at a 27% premium for a half-Mali asset.
AAUC.valley_of_death: The window IS the valley — there is no slow burn, just a single 37-day binary. Near-term path risk: (1) further NDRC silence into July keeps the spread wide and pins the stock; (2) any fresh Mali deterioration (junta action, attack, contract renegotiation) gives NDRC fresh cause to reject and pressures both the deal odds and the standalone floor; (3) headline-driven gap risk on any NDRC leak. There is no cash-burn hump, maturity wall, or dilution flagged — the company is cash-generative at record gold prices, so the floor is structural, not eroding. The only thing that 'decimates' value before the catalyst is an NDRC NO or a Jul-29 lapse, which takes the stock straight to the ~$23 floor (-10%).
AAUC.positioning_washout: Already partially washed out — the 6.5% drop to C$37.31 on the Jun-2 NDRC-delay news flushed some merger-arb holders and re-widened the spread, which is what creates the entry. The remaining holder base is arb desks underwriting the NDRC binary; a further negative NDRC headline could force another mechanical arb unwind toward the floor (the residual washout risk), while a clearance headline snaps the spread shut. No yield/passive base to force-liquidate; this is a pure event book.
AAUC.moderator_conclusion: AAUC is a textbook PENDING_HARD defined-risk merger-arb and exactly the setup this funnel is built to size INTO, not cap. The deal is signed, friendly, all-cash (C$44), board-recommended, with NO financing-out and NO CVR games; every approval EXCEPT China NDRC is already secured; and there is a hard, mutual-extension-only outside date of 2026-07-29 just 37 days out. Live $25.64 vs a ~$31 USD cash target with a real, gold-backed ~$23 standalone floor gives ~2x+ asymmetry with a capped ~10% downside. The reason I hold conviction at 4 rather than 5 is honest, not reflexive: the single load-bearing condition is a SOVEREIGN, non-economic gatekeeper (NDRC) that has ALREADY delayed the deal over the premium and Mali risk — a fatter, less-controllable binary than a routine antitrust clearance, with Mali concentration as a correlated single-point-of-failure. That is a real-but-priced risk, not a broken catalyst, so it earns an AGGRESSIVE-ENTRY A with conviction 4, sized for the binary. value_conviction is also 4: the margin of safety is the verified ~$23 break floor (hard, gold-ounce-backed, no solvency/dilution overhang) capping the loss near 10% against +21% upside — that defined downside, not a franchise moat, is what backstops the capital. Forcing function: NDRC decision by the Jul-29 wall. Position it as a sovereign-binary arb with a known floor, not a compounder.
AAUC.interrogator_dossier:
# AAUC — Allied Gold Corp. | Interrogator Dossier (Event-Driven Special Situation)

**Catalyst class:** Merger-arb / foreign-regulator close. **Super-cluster:** Deal-completion. **As of:** 2026-06-22. Live $25.64 (Jun-18 close) / $25.65 pre-mkt Jun-22 - Deal terms C$44.00 cash - Target ~ $31.08 USD (C$44 @ USD/CAD 1.4146) - Floor $23.00 - Hard date 2026-07-29 (37-42d).

This is NOT a quality/compounding thesis. AAUC is a single-asset-concentrated African gold miner being taken out for cash; the thesis is the SPREAD between $25.64 and the C$44 deal value, gated on the last remaining condition — China NDRC outbound-investment approval — firing before the July 29 outside date. The franchise is irrelevant; the catalyst carries the trade.

## 1. THE EVENT — REAL, DATED, BINDING?
REAL & SIGNED. On 2026-01-26 Allied Gold entered a definitive arrangement agreement to be acquired by Zijin Gold International (HK-listed gold arm of China's Zijin Mining) at C$44.00/share cash, ~C$5.5B equity, a friendly board-recommended all-cash offer — 27% premium to the undisturbed 30-day average. Binding plan-of-arrangement, not an LOI; court/shareholder approvals are non-issues for a friendly recommended cash deal; gating items are regulatory. DATED: Outside Date extended to 2026-07-29 (from ~May-2026), and per the 2026-05-29 release 'any further extension of the Outside Date will require the parties' mutual agreement' — a real hard wall, the forcing function. BINDING on ONE remaining condition: approvals SECURED = Investment Canada Act (completes Canadian review), ECOWAS competition, COMESA competition, African host-country approvals obtained/advanced. Single load-bearing outstanding condition = China NDRC outbound-investment approval.

## 2. THE LOAD-BEARING RISK — NDRC
The entire residual risk collapses to one binary: does Beijing's NDRC clear Zijin's outbound capital? Reporting (InvestingNews/NAI500/Ecofin, 2026-06-02) is explicit that NDRC DELAYED approval, questioning (a) the 27% premium Zijin is paying (outbound capital-outflow scrutiny) and (b) Mali political risk — Mali = ~half of Allied's output, junta has arrested foreign mining execs and forcibly renegotiated contracts. A third-party, single-binary, sovereign-discretion condition with non-economic motives — the worst kind for an arb. The May->July extension was driven by exactly this delay.

## 3. THE FLOOR
On the 2026-06-02 delay news AAUC fell 6.5% to C$37.31 (~$26.4 USD) — market did NOT mark to a break floor; base case still 'closes.' Break floor = standalone-miner value: undisturbed ~C$34-35 (C$44 = 27% premium to 30-day avg) ~ $24-25 USD, haircut for Mali risk, offset up by record gold prices. $23.00 USD floor is defensible and NOT zero: a break strips the premium, not the ounces. No going-concern, no covenant, no ATM/dilution flagged; cash-generative at current gold.

## 4. ASYMMETRY
Target ~$31.08 (+21%) vs floor $23.00 (-10%); raw R:R ~2.06-2.78x. Wide ~21% gross spread 37d from a hard outside date = market pricing non-trivial NDRC-fail probability. That is the source of edge and the reason it is not a layup.

## 5. RED/GREEN FLAGS
RED: NDRC sovereign non-economic gatekeeper actively flagging premium + Mali; Mali single-jurisdiction concentration (~50% output) drives both the discount and NDRC's hesitation (correlated); mutual-agreement-only outside date could lapse if NDRC isn't done by Jul-29. GREEN: friendly board-recommended ALL-CASH definitive agreement (no financing-out/CVR/stock-ratio); every non-China approval already in hand; record gold lifts floor + Zijin's motive; market held near deal value through the delay.

## 6. CREDIBILITY
Event maximally credible as to existence/terms (signed, friendly, cash, dated) but elevated single-condition completion risk (NDRC sovereign discretion over a half-Mali asset at 27% premium). Genuine PENDING_HARD dated catalyst inside the window with a defined floor; the binary is fatter than a routine arb. Moat/secular N/A (gold miner).

CREDIBILITY_SCORE: 4 | TRAJECTORY: STABLE | MOAT: NONE | MOAT_TREND: STABLE | SECULAR_THREAT: none

skeptic.kill_fact: I tried to kill this on three vectors and each held against a primary source. (1) CATALYST FIRED/SLIPPED? No — the deal is live, unbroken, and dated: Allied's own 2026-06-10 GlobeNewswire release describes 'Normal Course Progress with Zijin Gold Arrangement' (deal NOT closed and NOT broken as of mid-June), and the 2026-05-29 release (also filed as a 6-K) fixes the hard outside date at 2026-07-29 with the exact load-bearing language 'Any further extension of the Outside Date will require the parties' mutual agreement.' (2) TARGET REAL? Yes — C$44.00/share all-cash is the contractual consideration in a signed plan of arrangement (Jan-26 agreement, shareholders approved 2026-03-31); the target is the cash you receive, not a model. (3) FLOOR REAL? Yes — a break strips the 27% premium but not the gold ounces; no financing-out, no CVR, no going-concern/ATM flagged, so the standalone-miner floor is structural. The single residual binary (China NDRC outbound-investment approval, delayed over premium + Mali risk) is correctly identified as the ONLY outstanding gate and is a real-but-PRICED sovereign-discretion risk, not a disqualifier. The wide ~21% spread is the market paying for that live binary, exactly as the dossier claims. No hidden trading-through-terms, no second-request surprise, no fabricated floor.
skeptic.corrections: None load-bearing. Note the deal value in USD floats with USD/CAD (target ~$31.08 at 1.4146 is FX-sensitive, not a fixed number) and the NDRC decision can resolve at any moment before the 2026-07-29 wall — verify NDRC status at entry, as a clearance headline snaps the spread shut and a rejection takes it to the floor. Live price/FX should be refreshed at sizing.
evidence: Allied Gold, 'Allied Gold Advances Receipt of Regulatory Approvals... and Extends the Outside Date to July 29, 2026,' GlobeNewswire/6-K, 2026-05-29 — confirms ICA, ECOWAS, COMESA clearances obtained; new outside date 2026-07-29; 'Any further extension of the Outside Date will require the parties' mutual agreement.'

Allied Gold, 'Allied Gold Provides Update on Continued Value Creation... in Parallel with Normal Course Progress with Zijin Gold Arrangement,' GlobeNewswire, 2026-06-10 — confirms deal still live/unbroken and progressing as of mid-June 2026 (not closed, not terminated).
Allied Gold Corp Form 6-K (SEC EDGAR, CIK 0001993344) — definitive arrangement with Zijin Gold International at C$44.00/share cash (~C$5.5B), 27% premium; shareholders approved 2026-03-31.

Investing News Network / Financial Times, 'Chinese Regulator Delays Zijin Gold's US$4 Billion Allied Gold Takeover,' 2026-06-02 — NDRC delay is the sole outstanding gate, citing concerns over the premium paid and Mali (~half of output) geopolitical risk; the reason for the May→July outside-date extension.

catalyst.dated_milestone: 2026-07-29 mutually-hard outside date for deal completion (extended once from ~May-2026); the single outstanding condition is China NDRC outbound-investment approval. Canada/ICA, ECOWAS, COMESA, and African host-country approvals already obtained.
catalyst.binding_reason: Signed friendly all-cash C$44 Zijin takeover, every approval secured EXCEPT one sovereign gate (China NDRC), against a mutual-extension-only hard 2026-07-29 outside date 37 days out; ~21% gross spread to ~$31 USD with a hard gold-ounce-backed ~$23 floor (-10%), ~2x defined-risk. Skeptic CONFIRMED, cap 4 — pinned AT the cap: the single load-bearing binary is a non-economic capital-control gatekeeper that has already delayed once over the premium + Mali risk, a fatter, less-controllable binary than a routine clearance.

AMLX.bull_thesis: AMLX is a defined-risk clinical binary on the pivotal Phase 3 LUCIDITY readout of avexitide (GLP-1 receptor antagonist) in post-bariatric hypoglycemia (PBH), guided to Q3 2026. Enrollment completed March 2026, so the readout is now a mechanical data-lock function of an already-full n=78 trial — the date is binding, not enrollment-dependent. The drug already worked across FIVE prior PBH trials at the SAME 90 mg QD dose and SAME composite Level 2+3 hypoglycemia endpoint now in the Phase 3 (Phase 2b: 64% LS-mean reduction; Phase 2 PREVENT: 50-75% fewer rescues). H.C. Wainwright calls LUCIDITY 'quite overpowered' (>95% power for the demonstrated 50-65% effect); Stifel assigns 75% PoS. Avexitide holds FDA Breakthrough Therapy Designation in an orphan-scale indication (~160,000 U.S. patients) with NO approved competitor — a fast, premium-priced 2027 launch path (HCW models $815M 2033 sales). Street targets bracket the thesis: Mizuho $21, BofA $22, H.C. Wainwright $28 raised to $34. A positive readout re-rates toward $28+. Bounded downside: $279.8M cash (runway into 2028, no going-concern, no financing contingency) plus a live second program (AMX0114 antisense in ALS, Phase 1 LUMINA, ENCALS biomarker data June 2026). The catalyst carries the thesis.
AMLX.bear_thesis: The avexitide re-rate has ALREADY happened — AMLX ran from a 52-wk low of $4.80 to $18.61 (mkt cap +~297%), so at ~$15.67 much of the spread to a $21-$28 target is consumed and the entry is NOT a washout. A negative or ambiguous LUCIDITY readout craters the single asset that the entire valuation rests on; this is a one-shot, single-binary, single-asset event with n=78. Management's credibility carries a real scar: the prior franchise drug AMX0035/Relyvrio FAILED its confirmatory PHOENIX Phase 3 in ALS (2024) and was withdrawn — clinical replication is never guaranteed. Hard cash-per-share is only ~$2.53, well below the ~$4 'floor,' which leans on pipeline-option value the market could refuse to pay in a sentiment-crushing miss — a panic could overshoot toward $4-5 (the prior bottom). A successful readout likely triggers a dilutive raise to fund the 2027 launch (known overhang). Magnitude of downside-to-floor (~-74%) is comparable to upside-to-target (~+79%); only the ~62-75% PoS tilts the EV positive.
AMLX.sop_bull: Event-WIN value ~$28/share (board target; corroborated by street $21-$34 range — Mizuho $21, BofA $22, H.C. Wainwright $28->$34). Derived from de-risked avexitide NPV: ~160,000-patient orphan PBH market, no competition, BTD, premium orphan pricing, HCW ~$815M peak-ish 2033 sales, 2027 launch — risk-adjusted post-positive-Phase-3 (de-risked from ~60-75% PoS toward approval). Plus residual optionality from AMX0114 (ALS) and net cash. Round number ~$28.
AMLX.sop_bear: Verified downside FLOOR ~$4.00/share. Hard cash floor = $279.8M / ~110.6M sh = ~$2.53/share (Mar 31 2026; runway into 2028, no going-concern). The ~$4 clearing price = net cash + a haircut pipeline-option stub (residual avexitide indications + AMX0114 ALS program), consistent with the 52-wk low of $4.80 before the avexitide re-rate. A failed PBH readout does NOT zero the company but resets it to roughly its pre-catalyst trough.
AMLX.sop_breakdown: Probability-weighted base case using verified odds (win_prob ~0.62, within Stifel 75% / HCW ~60% range): 0.62 * $28 (event-win target) + 0.38 * $4 (verified near-cash floor) = $17.36 + $1.52 = ~$18.9 risk-neutral expected value. Discounting for the ~105-day binary timing, single-asset concentration, n=78 execution risk and post-success dilution overhang, the prudent base-case SoP anchors ~$13 (below live ~$15.67) — i.e. the market is already paying a modest premium to a disciplined probability-weighted fair value, which is why this is an asymmetry/timing play rather than a deep-discount-to-fair-value play. Components: avexitide PBH NPV (the swing factor, $28 win / ~$0 incremental on miss), AMX0114 ALS option stub, ~$2.53/sh net cash.
AMLX.risk_reward: From ~$15.67: upside to $28 target = +~79%; downside to $4 floor = -~74%. Magnitudes roughly symmetric (~1.07:1 reward:risk on price), but the ~62-75% probability of a positive overpowered readout tilts expected value clearly positive (board EV +29%, payoff 1.27x). Defined-risk: loss bounded by a credible near-cash floor.
AMLX.catalyst_summary: Pivotal Phase 3 LUCIDITY topline readout of avexitide in post-bariatric hypoglycemia, Q3 2026 (board hard date 2026-09-30, ~105d) — enrollment complete (Mar 2026) so the date is mechanical; live ~$15.67 vs ~$28 win target / ~$4 near-cash floor, ~62-75% PoS, defined-risk asymmetry.
AMLX.dated_milestone: LUCIDITY Phase 3 avexitide topline readout, Q3 2026 (guided, on track; hard board date 2026-09-30); enrollment completed March 2026 (n=78). Secondary near-term: AMX0114 (ALS) Phase 1 LUMINA Cohort-1 biomarker data at ENCALS, June 2026.
AMLX.forcing_function: The LUCIDITY Phase 3 topline data release in Q3 2026 (data-lock-driven, ~105 days). Because enrollment is already complete, the readout fires mechanically off the 16-week primary endpoint — a hard, binding clinical event with no third-party, financing, or regulatory-discretion contingency.
AMLX.consensus_delta: The street is NOT obviously looking the wrong way — this is a well-covered, consensus-bullish catalyst (Mizuho $21, BofA $22, H.C. Wainwright $28->$34; Stifel Buy/75% PoS), and the stock has already re-rated ~297% off its lows. The expectations-arbitrage edge is therefore MODEST, not extreme: the differentiated read is that an OVERPOWERED (>95% power) Phase 3 testing the SAME dose/endpoint/population that already produced a replicated 50-65% effect across five prior trials deserves a PoS at the high end of the street's 60-75% band, while the live ~$15.67 still leaves ~+79% to the $28 target — i.e. the market is pricing meaningful but not full success probability. The wrong-way assumption to fade is any residual 'Relyvrio-PTSD' discount: the PHOENIX/ALS failure was a different drug, mechanism, indication, and trial with no prior replicated PBH-grade signal; applying that scar to an overpowered confirmatory of a five-times-replicated effect over-discounts the binary.
AMLX.valley_of_death: Limited near-term financial risk: $279.8M cash, runway guided INTO 2028, ~$37-42M quarterly burn — no maturity wall, no covenant, no forced ATM before the readout, no going-concern flag. The genuine 3-9 month risk is the binary itself: a negative or ambiguous LUCIDITY topline in Q3 2026 would reset the stock toward the ~$4 pre-catalyst trough (-~74%). Secondary swing: ENCALS (June 2026) AMX0114 ALS biomarker data could inject volatility either way ahead of the main event. Post-success, a dilutive equity raise to fund the 2027 launch is a near-certain overhang that caps the immediate upside capture.
AMLX.positioning_washout: No yield/passive base to wash out — this is an event-driven biotech holder set (catalyst traders, specialist healthcare funds, retail momentum). The relevant mechanical dynamic is the OPPOSITE of a forced-seller setup: a positive readout forces underweight benchmark and generalist accounts to chase, plus likely index/ETF rebalancing as the de-risked market cap re-rates; a miss forces fast-money catalyst capital out hard toward the floor. The base is already positioned long into the print (re-rate done), so there is no pre-event capitulation flush to wait for.
AMLX.moderator_conclusion: AMLX is a textbook PENDING_HARD defined-risk special situation and earns an aggressive-entry verdict on the EVENT — NOT a compounder cap. The load-bearing catalyst is verified live: pivotal Phase 3 LUCIDITY topline for avexitide in PBH, guided Q3 2026 (board hard date 2026-09-30, ~105d), with enrollment ALREADY complete (Mar 2026) so the readout is a mechanical data-lock, binding, with no third-party/financing/regulatory-discretion contingency. The setup is high-quality: an overpowered (>95%) trial re-testing the exact dose/endpoint/population where avexitide already delivered a replicated 50-65% effect across five prior studies, BTD in a no-competitor orphan indication, ~62-75% PoS (Stifel 75%, HCW ~60%, board 0.62), and a corroborated $28 win target (street $21-$34). Downside is bounded by a credible near-cash floor (~$4 vs $2.53 hard cash, runway into 2028, no going-concern) plus a live second ALS program (AMX0114, ENCALS June 2026). I give it verdict A / conviction 4 (not 5): the catalyst is pristine, but the re-rate off the $4.80 low has already consumed much of the spread, so at ~$15.67 the price asymmetry is roughly symmetric in magnitude (+79% vs -74%) and only the PoS tilts EV positive (+29%) — a genuinely asymmetric, defined-risk bet, just no longer a washed-out entry, which is the one thing keeping it off a 5. value_conviction is 2: stripped of the catalyst, this is a no-revenue, negative-earnings, no-moat single-asset clinical biotech whose ~$13 prudent probability-weighted SoP sits BELOW the live price — there is no margin of safety to the verified downside floor ($4 floor is ~74% below spot), so the value case (ignoring the catalyst) is thin; the floor protects the loss but does not make the name cheap. Moat/secular N/A. Net: size it as a defined-risk catalyst position, not a value anchor.
AMLX.interrogator_dossier:
# AMLX - Amylyx Pharmaceuticals - Interrogator Dossier (Event-Driven Special Situation)

**Frame:** This is NOT a quality/compounding judgment. AMLX is a clinical-stage biotech with no approved product and negative earnings - cheap-and-bad on every franchise metric BY DESIGN. The thesis is the SPREAD between the live price (~$15.67, Jun 18 close; $15.88 pre-mkt Jun 22) and the event-resolved value of a HARD-DATED, data-driven binary: the pivotal Phase 3 LUCIDITY topline readout for avexitide in post-bariatric hypoglycemia (PBH), guided to Q3 2026 (board hard date 2026-09-30, ~105d). The interrogation is of the EVENT, not the moat.

## 1. IS THE CATALYST REAL, DATED, AND BINDING?
REAL - yes. LUCIDITY is a registrational, FDA-agreed pivotal Phase 3 (randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled), 78 participants, 3:2 avexitide 90 mg QD vs placebo, 16-week double-blind period, 21 U.S. sites, in adults with PBH following Roux-en-Y gastric bypass. Primary endpoint: reduction in composite Level 2 + Level 3 hypoglycemic events at Week 16. Avexitide carries FDA Breakthrough Therapy Designation - there is NO FDA-approved therapy for PBH (~160,000 U.S. patients, 8,000-12,000 new RYGB cases/yr).
DATED - yes, and tightening. Topline repeatedly guided to Q3 2026 ('on track,' reaffirmed Q1 2026 results, May 2026). Enrollment completed March 2026 - the clock to a 16-week-primary readout is now mechanical, not enrollment-dependent. Hard board date 2026-09-30 sits at the back edge of the guided Q3 window.
BINDING - yes. A clean clinical binary (data event), not a deal contingent on a counterparty, financing, or regulator's discretion. No second-request, no CVR, no financing contingency.

## 2. SPONSOR / MANAGEMENT CREDIBILITY
Mixed but improving, materially de-risked by design. Bear anchor: prior drug AMX0035 (Relyvrio) FAILED its confirmatory PHOENIX Phase 3 in ALS (2024) and was withdrawn - a real scar. BUT avexitide is structurally stronger: replicated signal across FIVE prior PBH trials (Phase 2b: 64% LS-mean reduction at 90 mg QD - the exact dose/endpoint now in LUCIDITY; Phase 2 PREVENT: 50%/75% fewer rescues); overpowered design (>95% power per H.C. Wainwright); Stifel 75% PoS, HCW ~60% approval prob; board win_prob 0.62. Management pivoted post-Relyvrio, raised cash, acquired a de-risked Phase-3-ready BTD asset.

## 3. VERIFIED DOWNSIDE FLOOR
Hard cash floor ~$2.53/share: $279.8M cash/ST-investments (Mar 31 2026) / ~110.6M wtd-avg shares; runway into 2028; no going-concern, no maturity wall, no covenant, no forced pre-event ATM. Realistic post-failure clearing ~$4.00 (board floor) vs 52-wk low $4.80: a PBH miss does not zero AMLX - it retains net cash, residual avexitide optionality, and a live second program AMX0114 (antisense, ALS), Phase 1 LUMINA, ENCALS biomarker data June 2026. The $4 floor = cash + haircut pipeline stub; the loss is bounded.

## 4. RED FLAGS
Single-binary, single-asset event; re-rate from $4.80 to $18.61 already priced meaningful success, so ~$15.67 is NOT a washed-out entry. Management's PHOENIX/Relyvrio failure; small n=78. Cash-per-share ($2.53) is below the $4 floor - the floor leans on pipeline-option value the market could refuse to pay on a sentiment-crushing miss. Post-success dilutive raise likely (known overhang).

## 5. GREEN FLAGS
Enrollment DONE (Mar 2026) -> mechanical date. Same dose/endpoint/population that worked across 5 trials; overpowered (>95%). BTD + zero-competition orphan -> fast premium launch (HCW $815M 2033 sales; launch 2027). Defined-risk: near-cash floor, no going-concern, clean binary. Street targets $21-$34 bracket the $28 board target.

## CAPITAL-ALLOCATION VERDICT
A genuine PENDING_HARD defined-risk special situation: binding, fully-enrolled, overpowered pivotal Phase 3 binary, Q3 2026 mechanical readout, corroborated $28 win target (street $21-$34), credible near-cash ~$4 floor. The director must weigh that the re-rate off the $4.80 low has ALREADY occurred - at $15.67 the asymmetry is GOOD but not pristine (+79% to $28 vs -74% to $4), carried by a ~62-75% PoS that tilts EV positive (+29%). Moat/secular N/A.

CREDIBILITY_SCORE: 4 | TRAJECTORY: STRENGTHENING | MOAT: NONE | MOAT_TREND: STABLE | SECULAR_THREAT: none

skeptic.kill_fact: No kill. Every load-bearing fact survives primary-source verification: LUCIDITY is fully enrolled (n=78, last patient randomized/dosed 2026-03-24 per company release), topline 'on track, anticipated Q3 2026' is reaffirmed in the Q1 2026 results, and the readout is a mechanical 16-week-primary data-lock with no financing/counterparty/regulatory-discretion contingency. The floor is real: $279.8M cash/marketable securities at 2026-03-31, runway guided INTO 2028, no going-concern, no forced pre-event ATM. The one disqualifier the bull under-weights is that the asymmetry is NOT a washout — the stock has already re-rated ~3x off the $4.80 low to ~$15.67, so price upside (+79% to $28) and downside (-74% to ~$4) are roughly symmetric in magnitude; only the ~62-75% PoS tilts EV positive. This is a genuine defined-risk binary, not a discounted entry, and it carries the verified Relyvrio/PHOENIX confirmatory-failure scar plus near-certain post-success dilution — so it earns aggressive-on-the-event sizing but NOT pristine conviction.
skeptic.corrections: (1) Bull's downside floor of $4.00 leans on a pipeline-option stub; HARD cash-per-share is only ~$2.53 ($279.8M / ~110.6M sh) — in a sentiment-crushing miss the clear can undershoot $4 toward the $4.80 prior trough or lower, so treat $4 as soft, $2.53 as the hard backstop. (2) Peak-sales anchor: bull cites HCW ~$815M (2033); current street is materially higher — BofA models ~$1.8B and Stifel >$1.5B peak — which strengthens, not weakens, the win-target. (3) Target precision: BofA = $22 (raised from $19), Stifel = $21 (Buy), HCW = $34, Mizuho = $21; the $28 board win target sits mid-range, well-corroborated. (4) Post-success dilutive raise to fund the 2027 launch is a confirmed known overhang that caps immediate upside capture.
evidence: Amylyx press release, 2026-03-24: 'Announces Completion of Enrollment in Pivotal Phase 3 LUCIDITY Clinical Trial of Avexitide' — last participant randomized and dosed, 78 participants, 16-week DB period, FDA-agreed composite Level 2+3 hypoglycemia primary endpoint, 'Topline data readout on track; anticipated in Q3 2026' (amylyx.com/news).

Amylyx Q1 2026 Financial Results, 2026-05-07 (8-K ex-99.1, SEC EDGAR CIK 0001658551 acc. 000119312526210286): $279.8M cash and marketable securities at 2026-03-31 (vs $317M at YE2025), runway guided INTO 2028 covering LUCIDITY Q3 2026 readout + potential 2027 approval/launch; no going-concern.

Analyst targets (Investing.com, Apr-May 2026): Mizuho $21 (Outperform), BofA $22 (peak sales ~$1.8B), Stifel $21 Buy (peak >$1.5B), H.C. Wainwright $34 — street range $21-$34 brackets the ~$28 win target.

Sponsor scar (verified): AMX0035/Relyvrio failed its confirmatory PHOENIX Phase 3 in ALS (2024) and was withdrawn — real replication risk, though avexitide is a different drug/mechanism/indication with a 5-trial replicated PBH signal at the same 90mg dose/endpoint.

catalyst.dated_milestone: LUCIDITY Phase 3 avexitide topline readout, Q3 2026 (guided, on track; hard board date 2026-09-30); enrollment completed March 2026 (n=78). Secondary near-term: AMX0114 (ALS) Phase 1 LUMINA Cohort-1 biomarker data at ENCALS, June 2026.
catalyst.binding_reason: Pivotal Phase 3 LUCIDITY readout (avexitide, PBH), guided Q3-2026, fully enrolled (mechanical data-lock), overpowered (>95%) re-test of a 5-times-replicated 50-65% effect, ~$4 near-cash floor. Skeptic CONFIRMED_WITH_CORRECTIONS at cap 68. The disqualifier the bull under-weights and the reason it is NOT aggressive: the re-rate off the $4.80 low to ~$15.67 is DONE, so price upside (+79%) and downside (-74%) are roughly SYMMETRIC in magnitude — only the ~62-75% PoS tilts EV positive. A genuine defined-risk binary, but not a washed-out entry; hard cash is only ~$2.53 (the $4 floor leans on a pipeline stub).

AQST.bull_thesis: Anaphylm (dibutepinephrine sublingual epinephrine film) is a genuinely differentiated, needle-free, orally-administered epinephrine for Type I allergic reactions/anaphylaxis in a large auto-injector market (EpiPen/neffy). The January 30, 2026 Complete Response Letter raised only NARROW, fixable deficiencies - human factors validation, one supportive PK study, and packaging/labeling/container-closure - with NO CMC, efficacy, or comparability concerns. The company completed an in-person Type A meeting with the FDA (March 2026) and aligned on both the PK and HF study designs, de-risking the path. It guides to resubmit the NDA in Q3 2026; on a Class 2 (6-month) review that points to an approval decision ~Q1 2027. Critically, the balance sheet was fixed pre-emptively: a $150M Oaktree facility (May 2026, $55M funded at close, +$20M on Anaphylm approval) plus ~$110M cash and FY26 revenue guidance of $46-50M remove going-concern/forced-dilution risk through the resubmission. A clean refile followed by approval re-rates the equity toward the $8.50 board target / $9.33-9.50 street targets, roughly double the live ~$4.26. The downside is cushioned by a REAL recurring revenue base (Sympazan/Libervant/PharmFilm royalties; Q1-26 revenue $14.4M, +66% y/y), so this is a funded, revenue-backed special situation, not a binary-or-bust shell.
AQST.bear_thesis: The load-bearing catalyst the board priced ALREADY FIRED AND FAILED: the original PDUFA was January 31, 2026 and the FDA issued a Complete Response Letter (rejection) on January 30, 2026 - the stock fell ~39%. The board's 'hard date 2026-09-30' is NOT an FDA decision; it is merely the company's guided NDA RESUBMISSION window, and the true approval decision is ~Q1 2027 and not yet clock-dated. So there is no 105-day binary - the real event is two gates away (clean resubmission, then a 6-month review) and the second gate is undated. This is a second-attempt regulatory bet: human-factors validation is a common re-trip hazard and a second CRL would be devastating. Management already missed once, took on senior debt at the trough (equity now junior to a larger Oaktree creditor), and Libervant's full US launch is gated to a competitor's Jan-2027 orphan-exclusivity expiry. If the resubmission slips or a second CRL lands, the stock reverts to the revenue-plus-cash floor (~$2.75, ~-35%), and the optionality the bull pays for evaporates. The asymmetry is real but mis-timed: the board's PENDING_HARD framing overstates imminence.
AQST.sop_bull: Event-win value. Approval re-rates AQST on Anaphylm's commercial potential in a large anaphylaxis market plus the existing royalty base. Board target $8.50; Wall Street targets $9.33-9.50 (Buy consensus, June 2026). Derivation: differentiated needle-free epinephrine peak-sales optionality (NPV) layered on the recurring Sympazan/Libervant/PharmFilm royalty stream (FY26 rev $46-50M), funded by ~$110M cash + the $150M Oaktree facility (incl. +$20M unlocked on approval). Implies ~$8.50-9.50/share, ~+100-120% from ~$4.26.
AQST.sop_bear: Verified downside floor ~$2.75. Derivation: Anaphylm written to a low probability (second CRL / indefinite slip), value = recurring commercial/royalty base (Sympazan owned + Libervant/PharmFilm royalties, ~$46-50M FY26 revenue capitalized at a distressed small-cap specialty multiple) + ~$110M cash LESS the enlarged Oaktree debt. This is the level the stock troughed to on the January CRL flush before bouncing. It is a fundamental, revenue-backed floor (not contractual deal cash), so breachable if the royalty base disappoints or a second rejection lands - ~-35% from the live price.
AQST.sop_breakdown: Probability-weighted base case using the verified path, NOT the board's 0.70 at-a-hard-date odds (that date is the resubmission, not the decision). Narrow, FDA-aligned CRL deficiencies imply a high but not certain eventual-approval probability (~0.60) on the ~Q1-2027 decision, discounted for the extra undated quarters and the resubmission-slip risk: ~0.55 effective -> 0.55 x $8.50 (approval) + 0.45 x $2.75 (floor) = $4.68 + $1.24 = ~$5.90 undiscounted; time-discounting ~3 quarters and the two-gate structure pulls base-case fair value to ~$5.25-5.75, midpoint ~$5.50. Above the live ~$4.26 but well below the approval target - the spread is real but the clock is longer and softer than the board models.
AQST.risk_reward: Upside-to-target ~+100% ($4.26 -> $8.50); downside-to-floor ~-35% ($4.26 -> $2.75). Raw asymmetry ~2.8:1. Base-case fair value ~$5.50 implies ~+29% to fair vs ~-35% to floor (~0.8:1) on a TIME-ADJUSTED, two-gate basis - attractive on the win-leg but the imminence the board assumes is absent; the favorable raw ratio is diluted by the ~Q1-2027, undated decision.
AQST.catalyst_summary: Anaphylm sublingual epinephrine: original Jan-31-2026 PDUFA FIRED as a CRL (rejection); NDA resubmission guided Q3 2026 (the board's '2026-09-30' = resubmission, NOT a decision), with the true FDA approval decision ~Q1 2027 on a 6-month clock - real, narrow-deficiency, funded second-attempt asymmetry, but undated and two gates out.
AQST.dated_milestone: NDA resubmission guided Q3 2026 (FDA-aligned PK + HF studies); post-resubmission Class 2 6-month review -> approval decision est. ~Q1 2027 (PDUFA not yet assigned). Final Type A meeting minutes received ~early May 2026.
AQST.forcing_function: The Q3 2026 NDA resubmission acceptance + FDA assignment of a new PDUFA action date - that is the event that converts this from SOFT_EXTENDED (undated) to PENDING_HARD (dated binary) and forces a re-rate. The terminal forcing function is the ~Q1-2027 FDA approval/CRL decision itself.
AQST.consensus_delta: Two-sided. The catalyst board is OFFSIDE optimistically - it carries a stale PENDING_HARD framing ($4.17->$8.50, win_prob 0.70, hard date 2026-09-30, EV +62%) that assumes a 105-day binary FDA decision. Verification shows that binary already fired as a CRL on Jan-30-2026 and the 2026-09-30 date is merely the resubmission window; the real decision is ~Q1-2027 and undated. Conversely, the street (Buy consensus, $9.33-9.50 targets, June 2026) is arguably correct on the EVENTUAL value but may under-weight resubmission-slip and second-CRL risk and the time value lost. The true expectations gap: the market post-CRL prices ~$4.26, between the $2.75 floor and the ~$8.50 approval case, i.e. roughly a coin-flip on a narrow-deficiency, FDA-aligned refile - which, given how benign the CRL deficiencies are (HF + 1 PK + packaging, no CMC/efficacy), is plausibly too pessimistic on the win-probability but correctly skeptical on timing.
AQST.valley_of_death: Manageable, materially de-risked vs a typical post-CRL small-cap. The usual 3-9 month killers - cash-burn hump and forced dilution between CRL and refile - were pre-empted by the $150M Oaktree refinancing (May 2026; $55M funded at close, repaid the prior $45M loan, improved interest-only terms) on top of ~$110M cash and FY26 revenue guidance of $46-50M, with an EBITDA loss guide of only ~$30-35M. So no near-term forced equity raise. Residual near-term risks: (1) the Q3-2026 resubmission slips if the HF/PK studies run long; (2) added senior debt service and equity subordination to Oaktree; (3) Libervant's full US launch is gated to a competitor's Jan-2027 orphan-exclusivity expiry, so the royalty ramp is back-loaded. None of these decimate FCF before the catalyst, but a resubmission slip would extend the undated wait and pressure the stock.
AQST.positioning_washout: The mechanical washout largely ALREADY HAPPENED on the Jan-30-2026 CRL (~-39%/-43% single-day moves cited), flushing the approval-front-runner base; the stock then re-based to ~$4 and has chopped $4.4-4.7 intraday into June. The remaining holders are event/biotech-catalyst specialists, not yield/passive holders, so there is no dividend-cut or index-driven forced seller left to clear. A residual washout risk is a SECOND disappointment (resubmission slip or a second CRL) that would force the catalyst base out toward the ~$2.75 floor - but the capitulation that usually precedes an aggressive entry is mostly behind, not ahead.
AQST.moderator_conclusion: AQST is a REAL, funded, differentiated special situation whose catalyst the board has MIS-DATED. The load-bearing PDUFA (Jan-31-2026) already fired as a Complete Response Letter (a miss), and the board's '2026-09-30 hard date' is actually the guided NDA resubmission window, not an FDA decision - the true approval/CRL decision is ~Q1-2027 on a 6-month Class 2 clock and is not yet assigned. That reclassifies the catalyst from PENDING_HARD to SOFT_EXTENDED: a single-binary, second-attempt regulatory event that is undated and two gates away (clean resubmission, then approval). Per catalyst-regime discipline, a SOFT_EXTENDED catalyst is mid-conviction - I do NOT award the aggressive-entry 5 the board's framing implies, because the imminence is not there. BUT the setup is materially better than a typical post-CRL name: the CRL deficiencies are narrow and non-fatal (human factors + one PK study + packaging; no CMC/efficacy), the FDA aligned on the fix at a Type A meeting, the balance sheet was repaired pre-emptively ($150M Oaktree + ~$110M cash, no forced dilution), and there is a REAL recurring revenue floor (~$46-50M FY26 royalty/product revenue) backing the ~$2.75 downside. Net: verdict B - WATCHLIST FOR THE RESUBMISSION. The thesis is alive and the asymmetry (raw ~2.8:1) is genuine, but the right entry is on/after the Q3-2026 resubmission acceptance + new PDUFA assignment, which converts this back to a dated PENDING_HARD binary worth aggressive sizing. Conviction 3 on the event (real but undated/soft, two-gate). value_conviction 4: the margin of safety to the VERIFIED floor is strong - the floor is backed by recurring revenue + a funded balance sheet (not deal cash), the going-concern overhang is removed, and the live ~$4.26 sits only ~35% above a credible, revenue-backed $2.75 - a defensible risk-defined entry even before the clock is re-dated. Base-case fair value ~$5.50.
AQST.interrogator_dossier:
# AQST - Aquestive Therapeutics - Catalyst Interrogation Dossier

**Event-driven special situation. Driver: Anaphylm (dibutepinephrine sublingual epinephrine film) FDA approval.** Verified live as of 2026-06-22. Live price ~$4.26.

## HEADLINE - THE BOARD FRAMING IS STALE
The original load-bearing catalyst already FIRED, and it FAILED. The FDA assigned a PDUFA action date of January 31, 2026; on January 30, 2026 Aquestive received a Complete Response Letter (CRL) - a rejection, not an approval. The stock fell ~39% (then bounced; the ~$4.26 print is the post-CRL re-baselined price). The board's 'HARD DATE 2026-09-30' is NOT an FDA decision date - it is the guided NDA RESUBMISSION window (Q3 2026). After a Class 2 resubmission, FDA takes a 6-month clock, so the actual decision lands ~Q1 2027, not September 2026. This reclassifies the catalyst from PENDING_HARD to SOFT_EXTENDED.

## IS THE CATALYST REAL, DATED, BINDING?
Real: yes - Anaphylm is a differentiated needle-free sublingual epinephrine; the CRL deficiencies are narrow and fixable (human factors, one supportive PK study, packaging/labeling/container-closure; NO CMC, efficacy, or comparability concerns). Dated: soft - 'Q3 2026' resubmission is company guidance, the post-resubmission PDUFA is unassigned (~Q1 2027 est.), and the 2026-09-30 board date is the resubmission mislabeled as a decision. Binding: the FDA review binds once a clock is assigned, but the resubmission is a company action that has slipped conceptually (CRL Jan -> Type A March -> minutes May -> resubmit Q3) and could slip again.

## SPONSOR / MANAGEMENT CREDIBILITY
Mixed-to-constructive. They MISSED the original approval (a CRL is a miss), but post-CRL execution is orderly: completed an in-person Type A meeting (March 2026) with FDA alignment on PK and HF study designs and acknowledgment of the improved container-closure; reaffirmed Q3-2026 resubmission at Q1 earnings; and shored up the balance sheet with a $150M Oaktree refinancing (May 12, 2026; $55M funded at close, repaid the prior $45M loan, +$20M on Anaphylm approval). With ~$110M cash and FY26 revenue guide $46-50M, no forced equity raise is needed. Credible on PROCESS; the timeline they sell understates the true distance to the binary.

## VERIFIED DOWNSIDE FLOOR (~$2.75)
Reasonable but SOFT (revenue-backed, not contractual). AQST is NOT a pre-revenue shell: it has marketed/royalty products - Sympazan (owned), Libervant (royalties; full US launch gated to a competitor's Jan-2027 orphan-exclusivity expiry), PharmFilm. Q1-2026 revenue $14.4M (+66% y/y); license+royalty $5.4M from $0.8M. FY26 guide $46-50M. ~$110M cash + $150M Oaktree facility - going-concern manageable. The $2.75 floor approximates the recurring royalty base + cash minus debt with Anaphylm written low; it held on the January CRL flush. Backed by recurring revenue + a funded balance sheet, so it is a fundamental (breachable) floor, ~35% below the live price.

## RED / GREEN FLAGS
RED: original catalyst FAILED (CRL); board hard date mis-specified (09-30 = resubmission, not decision); single-binary second-attempt risk; senior debt taken at the trough (equity junior to Oaktree). GREEN: CRL deficiencies narrow/addressable (HF + 1 PK + packaging; no CMC/efficacy); FDA alignment achieved at Type A; balance sheet fixed pre-emptively (no death-spiral dilution); real recurring revenue base (+66% y/y); differentiated asset, street targets $9.33-9.50 ~2x the live price.

## ASYMMETRY
Upside-to-target $4.26->$8.50 ~+100% (street $9.33-9.50 richer); downside-to-floor $4.26->$2.75 ~-35%. Raw ~2.8:1 - attractive IF imminent, but the up-leg now requires a clean Q3 resubmission AND a clean ~Q1-2027 approval (second gate undated), so time-adjusted asymmetry is weaker than the 105-day framing implies.

## VERDICT
Thesis alive but mis-timed. Real differentiated asset, fixable CRL, FDA-aligned path, funded balance sheet, revenue-backed floor - a legitimate special situation, but two gates and ~2 undated quarters away, not one hard date in 105 days. Size for SOFT_EXTENDED, not PENDING_HARD; the right entry is on the resubmission acceptance + new PDUFA assignment.

CREDIBILITY_SCORE: 3 | TRAJECTORY: STABLE | MOAT: NONE | MOAT_TREND: STABLE | SECULAR_THREAT: none

skeptic.kill_fact: The board's load-bearing PENDING_HARD catalyst already FIRED AND FAILED: the FDA issued a Complete Response Letter on Jan 30, 2026 (stock -~39%), confirmed by SEC 8-K. The '2026-09-30 hard date' is NOT an FDA decision - it is merely the company's guided NDA RESUBMISSION window (Q3 2026), and NO new PDUFA action date has been assigned. The true approval/CRL decision is two gates out (clean resubmission, then a ~6-month review) and is UNDATED (~Q1-2027 est.). So there is no 105-day dated binary - this is a SOFT_EXTENDED, single-binary, second-attempt FDA bet, not an imminent hard catalyst. The asymmetry is real and the floor is revenue-backed/funded, so the setup survives, but the imminence the board priced is absent.
skeptic.corrections: (1) catalyst_status PENDING_HARD -> SOFT_EXTENDED: the 2026-09-30 'hard date' is the company's Q3-2026 resubmission GUIDANCE, not an FDA action date; no PDUFA is assigned. (2) The real decision is undated (~Q1-2027 on a post-resubmission Class 2 clock), two gates away. BBIO.bull_thesis: BBP-418 (ribitol prodrug) for LGMD2I/R9 carries a hard FDA PDUFA target action date of 2026-11-27 with Priority Review granted and NO advisory committee scheduled. This is not a coin-flip pre-data binary: the pivotal Phase 3 FORTIFY study already read out POSITIVE in 2025, so the November decision is an approval gate on a met-endpoint, priority-review package -- approval-given-positive-Ph3 base rates are high, supporting the 0.85 win_prob. It would be the first-and-only therapy for LGMD2I/R9 (~7,000 patients U.S.+EU), with Jefferies modeling $1B+ peak sales and BMO $1.7B risk-unadjusted. Crucially, BBIO is no longer a single-binary pre-revenue biotech: acoramidis/Attruby is already approved and ramping fast ($180.6M U.S. Attruby + $9.5M royalties = $194.5M Q1 2026 revenue, royalties +$9.3M YoY off EU/Japan), with $940M cash and management guiding breakeven by end-2026/early-2027 and sustainable cash-flow positivity thereafter. A $500M buyback is live -- management is a buyer below intrinsic. Spot ~$66.80 vs analyst targets $95-$106 (median ~$95) gives ~55% upside on approval; the spread has NOT closed (stock $63->$67 over June). Approval re-rates the equity toward $102-105 as the second commercial product de-risks and encaleret (ADH1, NDA submitted May 2026) stacks as further near-dated optionality.
BBIO.bear_thesis: The November PDUFA could deliver a CRL or a minor extension (manufacturing/CMC or labeling), not a data failure -- but enough to spent the re-rate and knock the stock to the standalone-acoramidis floor. BBIO carries NEGATIVE shareholders' equity and a complex royalty-pharma/convertible capital structure, a legacy of years of hub-and-spoke R&D burn; that limits balance-sheet flexibility and is why the floor is ~$58 rather than higher. If BBP-418 disappoints AND the acoramidis ramp decelerates or competitive ATTR-CM pressure compresses pricing, the breakeven inflection slips and the equity re-rates down on the franchise alone. The bear case is bounded, however: $940M cash plus a profitable-by-2027 ATTR-CM franchise means even a CRL is not a zero -- the downside is a re-rate to the standalone franchise (~$58, -13%), not a wipeout.
BBIO.sop_bull: Event-win value ~$102-105/sh. Derived: standalone acoramidis/Attruby ATTR-CM franchise (rapidly ramping, ~$780M annualized off Q1's $194.5M and accelerating, multi-jurisdiction, ~6yr exclusivity, breakeven late-2026) supports a mid-$50s-to-$60s base; layering BBP-418 approval ($1B+ peak sales risk-unadjusted, first-and-only, orphan pricing) + encaleret optionality adds ~$40-50/sh of pipeline value. BBIO.sop_bear: Verified downside floor ~$58/sh. Derived: BBP-418 CRL removes the pipeline premium and re-rates the equity to the standalone acoramidis franchise + net cash -- $940M cash/marketable securities, an approved ramping ATTR-CM product crossing to breakeven by end-2026/early-2027, partially offset by negative shareholders' equity and the royalty/convertible capital structure. Floor is franchise-backed (a profitable cardiomyopathy business), NOT a cash-shell liquidation value -- hence ~-13% to spot, not a zero.
BBIO.sop_breakdown: Probability-weighted: 0.85 x $103 (approval/target) + 0.15 x $58 (CRL/floor) = ~$87.6, rounded toward a conservative base-case ~$90-92 to account for timing and a haircut on the full $105 target. Parts: standalone acoramidis ATTR-CM franchise + $940M net cash anchor the ~$58 floor; BBP-418 approval adds ~$40-45/sh of pipeline value; encaleret (ADH1) is unpriced optionality on top.
BBIO.catalyst_summary: BBP-418 (LGMD2I/R9) FDA PDUFA hard-dated 2026-11-27 -- Priority Review, no AdCom, positive Ph3 FORTIFY behind it; ~$67 spot vs ~$105 target with a franchise-backed ~$58 floor = ~4:1 asymmetric, defined-risk.
BBIO.dated_milestone: FDA PDUFA target action date for BBP-418 (LGMD2I/R9): 2026-11-27 (NDA accepted, Priority Review granted, no advisory committee scheduled).
BBIO.forcing_function: The 2026-11-27 BBP-418 PDUFA decision -- a hard FDA-committed date inside the window. Approval forces a re-rate toward the $95-106 analyst cluster as a second commercial product de-risks; a CRL caps loss at the franchise-backed ~$58 floor.
BBIO.consensus_delta: The Catalyst board itself mislabeled the load-bearing event as 'acoramidis PDUFA' -- acoramidis is ALREADY approved and the revenue engine; the live 2026-11-27 PDUFA is BBP-418 (LGMD2I/R9). The street is correctly bulled on BBP-418 (median PT ~$95, up to $106) but the stock at ~$67 still trades a ~30-55% discount to those targets, implying the market is pricing meaningful CRL/timing risk into a decision that sits on a POSITIVE Phase 3 + Priority Review + no AdCom. The other under-appreciated leg is the floor: many treat BBIO as a binary biotech, but it is now a profitable-by-2027 ATTR-CM franchise with $940M cash and a $500M buyback -- the downside is franchise-backed, not a zero. Consensus is not wrong-way, but the spread has not compressed to reflect how de-risked the approval gate is.
BBIO.valley_of_death: Limited. $940M cash and an approved, fast-ramping acoramidis franchise crossing to breakeven by end-2026/early-2027 remove a cash-burn cliff before the November catalyst. The real near-term risks are (1) a CRL or minor PDUFA extension at 2026-11-27 (CMC/labeling, not data), which re-rates to the ~$58 floor; (2) negative shareholders' equity / royalty-convertible capital structure limiting flexibility if the ramp stumbles; (3) ATTR-CM competitive/pricing pressure decelerating the Attruby ramp. None of these is a going-concern event inside the window.
BBIO.positioning_washout: Not a yield/passive base subject to mechanical liquidation -- this is a growth/event biotech holder set. The relevant flow is the opposite: a positive 2026-11-27 approval is a re-rating/short-covering catalyst as a second commercial product de-risks and index/peer flows follow a path-to-profitability biotech. The live $500M buyback provides a mechanical bid under the stock pre-event. No forced-seller overhang identified.
BBIO.moderator_conclusion: BBIO is a near-textbook aggressive-entry, defined-risk special situation -- with one correction: the load-bearing catalyst is BBP-418 (LGMD2I/R9), NOT acoramidis (already approved and now the revenue engine and floor). The BBP-418 PDUFA is hard-dated 2026-11-27, inside the window, Priority Review, no AdCom, and sits on a POSITIVE Phase 3 FORTIFY readout -- so the 0.85 win_prob is an approval-given-positive-data gate, not a data coin-flip. Spot ~$67 vs a verified ~$105 target (analyst cluster $95-$106) against a FRANCHISE-BACKED ~$58 floor ($940M cash + a profitable-by-2027 ATTR-CM business, $500M buyback live) yields ~4:1 asymmetry with a real, non-zero downside. This is exactly the setup the funnel exists to find; the compounder moat-erosion cap does NOT apply. Verdict A, conviction 5. value_conviction 4: the margin of safety to the verified $58 floor is strong (real commercial franchise + net cash), shaded one notch below 5 only for the negative shareholders'-equity / complex royalty-convertible capital structure flag. Moat/secular axes are largely N/A for an event trade; insofar as they apply, acoramidis carries orphan/regulatory exclusivity (~6yr) -- a narrow but real moat on the floor asset, no secular threat.
BBIO.interrogator_dossier:
# BBIO -- BridgeBio Pharma -- Interrogator Dossier (Event-Driven Special Situation)

**Catalyst class:** Bio binary -- FDA approval decision (PDUFA). Live price ~$66-67; target ~$102-105; floor ~$58.

## 0. CATALYST CORRECTION (load-bearing)
The Catalyst-Watch board labeled the load-bearing event 'acoramidis PDUFA.' That is wrong and must be corrected. Acoramidis (Attruby / BEYONTTRA) is ALREADY approved and commercially launched in the U.S., E.U., Japan, Switzerland, Brazil, and U.K. -- it is the revenue engine, not a pending decision. The actual hard-dated 2026-11-27 PDUFA belongs to BBP-418 (ribitol prodrug) for LGMD2I/R9. Verified on BridgeBio's 2026-05-27 press release (GlobeNewswire): FDA Acceptance and Priority Review of NDA for BBP-418, PDUFA target action date November 27, 2026, Priority Review granted, no advisory committee planned. The catalyst is REAL and DATED -- it is simply a different drug than the board recorded.

## 1. IS THE CATALYST REAL, DATED, AND BINDING?
Real & dated: Yes. NDA accepted, Priority Review, hard PDUFA 2026-11-27 (163 days out, inside window). No AdCom -- removes a public-vote wildcard. Clinical backing: BBP-418's Phase 3 FORTIFY read out POSITIVE (2025); the company filed off a met-endpoint package -- this is a regulatory-approval gate on positive data, not a coin-flip, which is why win_prob 0.85 is defensible. First-and-only therapy for LGMD2I/R9, ~7,000 patients U.S.+EU. Binding: a PDUFA date is an FDA commitment, not a management aspiration.

## 2. SPONSOR / MANAGEMENT CREDIBILITY
High. BridgeBio is a proven FDA executor -- it just took acoramidis from Ph3 to multi-jurisdiction approval and a fast ramp ($180.6M U.S. Attruby in Q1 2026 + EU/Japan royalties). They are stacking catalysts: encaleret NDA (ADH1) submitted May 2026 (potential priority review, launch early 2027) -- a second near-dated shot.

## 3. VERIFIED DOWNSIDE FLOOR
The floor is a real commercial franchise + net cash, not deal-break cash. $940.2M cash/marketable securities (Q1 2026), 'at least 6 years of runway before generics.' Acoramidis/Attruby is approved and fast-ramping: $194.5M Q1 2026 revenue ($180.6M U.S. Attruby + $9.5M royalties, +$9.3M YoY). Breakeven guided by end-2026/early-2027. $500M buyback live. Implication: even a CRL at the November PDUFA leaves a profitable-by-2027 ATTR-CM franchise with $940M cash. The ~$58 floor (~-13%) is a re-rate-to-standalone level, not liquidation. Floor is REAL and franchise-backed.

## 4. RED FLAGS
Negative shareholders' equity (legacy R&D burn + royalty monetization) -- limits flexibility, the main reason the floor is $58 not higher; mitigated by $940M cash + profit inflection. Complex royalty/convertible capital structure -- watch covenants, not going-concern; NOT an ATM-into-the-event flag. CRL / minor PDUFA extension is the live failure mode (CMC/label), not data. No AdCom (positive). Buyback under the event (bullish).

## 5. ASYMMETRY
Spot ~$66.80. Target ~$102-105 (~+55%; corroborated -- median analyst PT ~$95, Mizuho $106, Wells $98, HCW $100; Jefferies/BMO $1.1B-$1.7B BBP-418 peak sales). Floor ~$58 (~-13%). Upside-to-target ~4x downside-to-floor. Spread has NOT closed (stock $63->$67 over June).

## 8. EVENT-CREDIBILITY VERDICT
PENDING_HARD, dated 2026-11-27, binding, backed by positive Ph3, priority review, no AdCom, credible serial-approver management, and a franchise-backed floor. Near-textbook aggressive-entry special situation. Acoramidis carries orphan/regulatory exclusivity (~6yr) -- a narrow but real moat on the floor asset.

CREDIBILITY_SCORE: 5 | TRAJECTORY: STRENGTHENING | MOAT: NARROW | MOAT_TREND: STABLE | SECULAR_THREAT: none
